name: protocolsio-integration
description: Integration with protocols.io API for managing scientific protocols. This skill should be used when working with protocols.io to search, create, update, or publish protocols; manage protocol steps and materials; handle discussions and comments; organize workspaces; upload and manage files; or integrate protocols.io functionality into workflows. Applicable for protocol discovery, collaborative protocol development, experiment tracking, lab protocol management, and scientific documentation.
qualityReason: Specific API integration with use cases
capabilities: Search and retrieve scientific protocols by keywords, DOI, or category
Create, update, and publish protocols with full metadata and DOI assignment
Manage protocol steps, materials, and reagents programmatically
Organize team workspaces with role-based permissions and folder hierarchies
Handle discussions, comments, and collaborative feedback on protocols
Upload and manage files, images, and experimental data associated with protocols
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All 663 static findings are false positives from markdown code examples. The analyzer misinterpreted documentation containing API endpoint examples and HTTP request patterns as security issues. No security risks present.
valueStatement: This skill provides comprehensive integration with protocols.io API for managing scientific protocols. Use it to search existing protocols, create new protocols, manage collaborative workspaces, handle discussions, and organize laboratory documentation workflows.
userTitle: Search, create, and manage scientific protocols on protocols.io
limitations: Requires valid protocols.io API access token for authenticated requests
Rate limits apply (100 requests per minute for standard endpoints)
Does not provide authentication token generation; users must obtain their own credentials

useCases.title: Discover and cite protocols
useCases.description: Find existing protocols in your field, review community feedback, and properly cite protocols using DOIs in your research publications.

useCases.title: Organize team protocols
useCases.description: Set up shared workspaces for your lab team, upload experimental data, and track protocol executions across team members.

useCases.title: Export protocol data
useCases.description: Back up protocol collections, export workspace data for archival, and integrate protocols.io data into larger research pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Search protocols
promptTemplates.prompt: Find CRISPR protocols on protocols.io that include details about cell culture and transfection steps

promptTemplates.title: Create protocol
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new protocol on protocols.io for western blotting with detailed steps and required reagents

promptTemplates.title: Manage workspace
promptTemplates.prompt: List the protocols in my lab workspace and show me how to upload a new experimental data file to the protocols.io workspace

promptTemplates.title: Generate PDF
promptTemplates.prompt: Find my PCR protocol and generate a PDF version for offline reference and laboratory notebook documentation

bestPractices: Store API tokens securely in environment variables, never in code or version control
Implement exponential backoff to handle rate limit errors gracefully
Validate protocol data before API calls and use content_format parameter to get results in your preferred format
antiPatterns: Hardcoding API tokens directly in example code or scripts
Ignoring HTTP error codes and not implementing proper error handling
Exceeding rate limits by making too many requests without implementing backoff

faq.question: How do I get a protocols.io API token?
faq.answer: Obtain an access token from protocols.io settings. Use CLIENT_ACCESS_TOKEN for personal access or OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN for multi-user applications.

faq.question: What rate limits apply to API requests?
faq.answer: Standard endpoints allow 100 requests per minute. The PDF endpoint is limited to 5 requests per minute for signed-in users.

faq.question: Can I access private protocols?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper authentication you can access your private protocols and shared protocols based on your workspace permissions.

faq.question: How do I publish a protocol with a DOI?
faq.answer: Create the protocol first, add all required steps and materials, then call the publish endpoint to issue a DOI for your protocol.

faq.question: Can I export protocols to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, protocols can be exported in multiple formats including PDF, HTML, and JSON using the content_format parameter.

faq.question: How do workspace permissions work?
faq.answer: Workspaces support role-based access. Members can have different permissions for viewing, editing, and managing protocols within the workspace.
